title: The stimulus generator was selected for penalty magnitude, and the claim-grade cross-model numbers rest on that Claude-shaped distribution
evidence: Prereg quote verbatim: docs/preregistration_tierB.md lines 27-31 list 'penalty magnitude on screened-in pairs' as a generator-selection basis; lines 64-66 fix 'generator (haiku vs opus/sonnet Tier A) enters as a fixed effect in pooled models'. Recomputed absence: data/model_stats.json contains zero occurrences of 'generator' and no Tier A/B split (per_model blocks carry only n_rows/penalty/flips/sign_test), yet it pools Tier A + Tier B (gemma-2-2b n_rows 1027, n_phrases 687 vs the 132-pair Tier A set, prereg line 15) and feeds the page labeled 'The claim-grade table' (technical/index.html:392) with 'Every band sits below zero' (technical/index.html:355). Engine scripts/paired_stats_rigor.py and paired_stats.py have no generator handling either; the only executions of the guard are mentions (prereg, docs/critic/critic_20260711.md:31-33). methods.html limitations bullet 6 (:526) discloses 'The stimuli are constructed' but no site page discloses the penalty-magnitude selection criterion (site-wide grep: no hit for it outside the engine prereg).

title: Translation 'restores it' is an uncontrolled Claude round-trip evaluated on a downgrade-selected set
evidence: Confirmed: index.html:312 lede 'rewriting the sentence in clinical terms restores it'; translation/index.html:202 subtitle; prereg lines 46-48 'Translation panel: only on flagged downgrades' (selection is real); paraphrase_robustness mean |delta| 0.0642 (data/provenance.json); no translation panel exists on the hand-built set (trace_out/imported_pairs summaries have translation_method null, no translated prompts); generator/translator/auditor are all Claude family (methods.html:375-386; data/model_evaluations.json ids all claude-*). CORRECTIONS: (1) 'No placebo' is wrong — the placebo-paraphrase translation arm ran 2026-07-11 (trace_out/pairs_20260711T051145Z_txplacebo, translation_method 'llm_placebo', 10 chunk summaries); its rows are in the site's own data/urgency_shift.json (26 rows; recomputed mean urgency_recovery 0.028 vs 0.080-0.122 for the three real-translation arms) and it is analyzed in the engine's public docs/findings_synthesis_DRAFT_20260713.md section 5 (haiku 0.091 / opus 0.102 / placebo 0.033 tiers regained — ~2/3 of the benefit vocabulary-specific). The technical/index.html:331 'queued' bullet queues the LENS analysis of the three arms, not the arms themselves; the landed placebo supports specificity rather than undermining recovery. (2) The church-and-state paragraph already names Claude models as both test-writer and translator (methods.html:375-379) and the next paragraph names the auditor as 'a Claude model' (:385-386), so the requested disclosure largely exists. What survives: no site PAGE renders the placebo-translation result, and the present-tense front-page claim carries no caveat or link to it.

title: A published 45%→41% re-trace discrepancy contradicts the 'zero repeat variation' repeatability claim
evidence: Confirmed: methods.html:220-229 ('every repeat reproduced identical probabilities… Zero repeat variation has been observed so far'), translation/index.html:208 ('45% in the July 7 study trace, 41% on the July 9 re-trace') and :562 ('Independent re-trace of the study case'), data/retrace_consistency.json prob_spread_max 0.0 over 68 pairs, and the scope claim: scripts/retrace_consistency.py compares only clinical/patient panels (lines 46-63, 82-101) — translated panels are outside the 0-differences record, unstated on methods.html. CORRECTION: the two artifacts are NOT mutually exclusive, and the published record DOES determine which branch happened — the engine repo's trace files show the July 9 run traced a DIFFERENT translated sentence: trace_out/grandma_r2/batch_summary.part_01.json prompts.translated = 'Grandma has been constipated for a week…' (p=0.409) vs trace_out/urgency_downgrades_20260707T1/batch_summary.part_16.json prompts.translated = 'Grandmother has been constipated for a week…' (p=0.453), while the clinical panel reproduced exactly (0.263 = 0.263) and the patient top was identical (nap 0.299). So 'zero repeat variation' is not falsified; the finding's second branch is what occurred: 'independent re-trace of the study case' (translation page) and 're-trace jitter' (provenance.json retrace_20260709 note) mislabel what is, on the translated panel, a paraphrase comparison — which provenance.json half-concedes by pointing at paraphrase_robustness. The same-day scope point on the designed 60-trace sample matches docs/repeatability_sample.md (all three CI runs 2026-07-13), though the pooled 68-pair record does span 07-07 to 07-13.

title: Post-registration model additions presented at claim grade; a marginal exploratory result (medgemma) promoted into the headline narrative
evidence: Prereg fixes measurement on four models (docs/preregistration_tierB.md lines 38-40: gemma-2-2b, gemma-3-4b-it, qwen3-4b, qwen3-1.7b); docs/prereg_divergence_log.md row 3: 'extra models are secondary/exploratory; primary endpoints stay on the pre-registered four'. Site: technical/index.html ev-table builder (:917-932) renders all eight models identically — no 'exploratory/post-registration' tag, and 'divergence' appears on no site page (grep, modes/ excluded); endnote :598-603 attaches 'claim-grade interim numbers, with a pre-registered holdout still sealed' to the whole Part 4. Recomputed medgemma-4b-it from data/model_stats.json: n=119, mean -0.0339, CI95 [-0.068, -0.002] (upper bound 0.20 pp from zero; the report's '[-6.9, -0.2] pp' and '0.22 pp' are trivially off from -6.8 and 0.20), sign test 9v2 with p_bh 0.07478 (non-significant after correction, matching 'q=0.075'); probed 2026-07-13 per the divergence log. methods.html:514 carries the sentence 'The one medically tuned model measured, medgemma-4b-it… still shows a below-zero penalty' with no interim-CI or exploratory qualifier.

title: Front-door per-pair numbers violate the prereg's own paraphrase-noise rule, and the flagship is an undisclosed extreme-tail pick
evidence: Prereg line 66: 'no per-pair claim is reported without its paraphrase-noise caveat'. data/provenance.json paraphrase_robustness: mean_abs_delta_vs_baseline 0.0642, per_baseline_sd_median 0.0415, verdict 'single pairs are illustrations, inference lives in the paired aggregate' — rendered on zero pages; the only 'paraphrase' occurrence in page HTML outside modes/ is technical/index.html:331, a queued-work bullet. Uncaveated flagship per-pair numbers verified: index.html:18 og:image:alt ('inhaler at 69% … falls to 4%'), wording-differences/index.html:192 ('Language Penalty: −65% probability (0.69 → 0.04)'), phrase-dataset/index.html:124 ('a plumber — at 68%'), translation grandma case. Recomputed tail ratio: scenario 85 penalty 0.043−0.692 = −64.9 pp vs pooled gemma-2-2b mean −3.48 pp CI [−4.55, −2.43] (model_stats.json) ≈ 18.7× ('~19×' checks). Ranking disclosed only on simulated-scenarios/index.html:327 ('25 largest-effect scenarios (prediction flips first, then largest penalty)'); the front-page lede (:312, 'Try the live case below') has no adjacent base-rate anchor — a mean-penalty stat does exist on index.html but several sections lower (#sim-stat, :430/:740), not beside the demo. Nuance, not a defect in the premise: the rule dates to the 2026-07-09 Tier B prereg while the flagship cases are earlier Tier A/hand-built measurements, so 'breach' of the letter is arguable in scope; the absence of any per-pair noise caveat site-wide is factual. Related recompute requested by the caller (belongs to the MINOR version-anchor finding, not counted here): data/convergence.json series do include the emergency batches — every model's final point is through_batch pairs_20260713T135755Z, with pairs_20260713T031252Z also present (gemma-2-2b final n=650), the two batches data/provenance.json emergency_sets marks 'kept outside the pre-registered run'; that cited premise checks out.
